content.text:
Based on my visual inspection of the molecule, I can now clearly identify all the neighbour atoms of C:13.

## Results:

**Central Atom:** C:13 (Carbon with index 13)

**Neighbour Atoms of C:13:**

| Atomic Index | Element |
|--------------|---------|
| 12 | S (Sulfur) |
| 16 | C (Carbon) |
| 14 | H (Hydrogen) |
| 15 | H (Hydrogen) |

**geomconf_index:** 3915

**Summary:** Atom C:13 is bonded to 4 atoms: sulfur (S:12), carbon (C:16), and two hydrogen atoms (H:14 and H:15). This forms a -CH₂- group that is part of a thioether linkage, with C:13 being connected to the sulfur (S:12) on one side and the carbonyl carbon (C:16) on the other side.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 13, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
